channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: American figure skater Alysa Liu takes the lead on the opening night of the world championships
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — American figure skater Alysa Liu assumed the lead after the short program on the opening night of the world championships on Wednesday, proving that the Olympian is much farther along in her comeback from retirement than almost anyone thought.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Dem lawmaker suggests females athletically equal to males while voting to keep trans athletes in girls sports
item.description: Pennsylvania Democrat state senator Lindsey Williams suggested that female athletes are physically comparable to male athletes and don't face disadvantages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soto, Snell and Holmes debut for new teams, Skenes makes his first opening-day start
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Juan Soto, Blake Snell and Clay Holmes debut for new teams, Paul Skenes makes his first opening-day start at age 22 and Sandy Alcántara returns from Tommy John surgery.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Retired Texans star J.J. Watt named game analyst for NFL on CBS this season, pairing with Ian Eagle in move from studio
item.description: Retired Texans NFL Defensive Player of the Year J.J. Watt moves from studio to become CBS Sports' No.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Wilson, KPRC 2

item.title: Major League Cricket coming to Oakland Coliseum
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- In the East Bay, the City of Oakland has announced that Major League Cricket will play matches at the Coliseum this summer. Cricket is the world’s second most popular sport, after soccer.
item.dc:creator: Charles Clifford, KRON4

item.title: Investigation focus in death of ex-Yankees star's son shifts to possible food poisoning
item.description: Costa Rican investigators have shifted their focus in their investigation into the death of Miller Gardner to possible food poisoning. Miller was 14.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FIFA details Club World Cup prize money with $125M target for the winner in $1BN fund
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The winner of FIFA’s first 32-team Club World Cup in the United States could earn $125 million as details of a $1 billion prize money fund were finally published Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Arkansas' Calipari not backing off relying on freshmen in NIL, transfer portal era
item.description: (AP) - John Calipari embraced the one-and-done era like few college basketball coaches, becoming one of the best annually at bringing in talented freshmen and routinely getting them to Final Fours before shipping them off to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In the era of big velocities, some pitchers are still finding other ways to succeed
item.description: SURPRISE, Ariz. (AP) — Texas Rangers left-hander Cody Bradford says he realized early on that he probably wouldn’t ever possess the type of fastball that causes scouts to take a second look at their radar guns.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thunder become 1st team to reach 60 wins in NBA this season
item.description: The Oklahoma City Thunder became the first team in the NBA this season to win 60 games with a victory over the Sacramento Kings on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blues post 7 consecutive wins in a push to make the playoffs after a two-year absence
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — The wins just keep on coming for the St. Louis Blues, and their seven-game streak has significantly improved their chances of returning to the playoffs after a two-year absence.
item.dc:creator: WARREN MAYES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A handful of March Madness coaches stand out for their fashion choices amid a sea of casual attire
item.description: As the NCAA Tournament heats up, the styles of Notre Dame's Niele Ivey, LSU’s Kim Mulkey, Alabama’s Nate Oats, South Carolina’s Dawn Staley and a handful of other coaches stand out in a sea of coaches in team polo shirts and quarter-zip pullovers.
item.dc:creator: Kristie Rieken, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alex Ovechkin scores his 889th career goal to move 6 goals away from breaking Gretzky's NHL record
item.description: WINNIPEG, Manitoba (AP) — Alex Ovechkin scored his 889th career goal and had to go right by Scott Arniel and the Winnipeg Jets to celebrate with his Washington Capitals teammates. He didn't let the opportunity go to waste.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday. The team released a statement Tuesday afternoon that said it would visit the White House "in keeping with long-standing baseball tradition.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Jets quarterback Zach Wilson not ready to accept journeyman label ahead of new chapter in Miami
item.description: Miami Dolphins quarterback Zach Wilson said Monday that he's hopeful an opportunity to serve as NFL starter is still in the cards for him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Warriors' Stephen Curry won't play in Miami, 2nd straight game missed with pelvic contusion
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Stephen Curry is going to take a little more time to heal. Curry remained out of Golden State's lineup Tuesday night for a game against the Miami Heat, the second straight game he has missed with a pelvic contusion.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Same old Cal: Arkansas' Calipari not backing off relying on freshmen in NIL, transfer portal era
item.description: John Calipari embraced the one-and-done era like few college basketball coaches, becoming one of the best annually at bringing in talented freshmen and routinely getting them to Final Fours before shipping them off to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, WGN 9

item.title: Colorado State's Niko Medved leaves for Minnesota after 7-year run with Rams
item.description: Niko Medved, the second-winningest coach in Rams' history, has accepted the head coaching job with the Big Ten's Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Sam Merrill has found a home in Cleveland
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a homecoming for former Bountiful High and Utah State star Sam Merrill Sunday night. Merrill hit a couple of three-pointers in his return to his home state in Cleveland's 120-91 win over the Jazz. Merrill says loves playing in front of family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Late boxing legend George Foreman had deep ties to this East Bay city
item.description: (KRON) -- Boxing legend and former heavyweight champion George Foreman died last Friday at the age of 76. Foreman was born in Texas. However, after a troubled upbringing, he then moved to the West Coast after ninth grade when he enrolled in President Lyndon B. Johnson's Job Corps work program.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: San Diego FC dealt first loss in inaugural season
item.description: San Diego FC lost its first game of the season thus far on Sunday when the club fell 2-1 to Austin FC at Q2 Stadium in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Koby Brea leads the way as Kentucky beats Illinois 84-75 for first Sweet 16 appearance since 2019
item.description: Koby Brea matched a career high with 23 points and Kentucky beat Illinois 84-75 on Sunday to advance beyond the NCAA Tournament’s opening weekend for the first time since they reached the Elite Eight in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms his relationship with Vanessa Trump in a social media post
item.description: Tiger Woods has confirmed his relationship with the former daughter-in-law of President Donald Trump, publishing two images to social media in a post that said, “Love is in the air.” Woods and Vanessa Trump, who was married to Donald Trump Jr.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New York girls basketball coach fired for hair-pulling incident with player apologizes
item.description: In a statement shared with local media, Zullo, 81, said he regretted his behavior and offered apologies to the player, her family, the team, the district, and the community.
item.dc:creator: Griffin Haas, NewsNation

item.title: LeBron James' return from injury spoiled by Bulls' phenomenal effort in 146-115 win over Lakers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James' return from a seven-game absence was the dominant storyline, to say the least, when the Bulls got ready to face the Lakers in Los Angeles on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC wins scoring fest over Tampa Bay, 6-4
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Logan Cooley and Alex Kerfoot each scored twice and the Utah Hockey Club defeated the Tampa Bay Lightning 6-4 at Delta Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: March Madness: No. 2 UConn routs Arkansas State 103-34 in opener as Fudd has 27 points
item.description: After the Red Wolves’ Crislyn Rose scored the game’s first basket in the NCAA Tournament first-round game, UConn ripped off the next 22 points en route to a convincing 103-34 victory on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JIM FULLER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Purdue ends McNeese's March Madness run 76-62 to get back to Sweet 16
item.description: Trey Kaufman-Renn had 22 points and 15 rebounds, and Purdue used a fast start to roll to a 76-62 win over McNeese in the second round of the NCAA Tournament on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers-Cubs opener in Tokyo averages more than 25 million viewers in Japan, a record audience
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tuesday's first game of the Tokyo Series averaged more than 25 million viewers in Japan according to Major League Baseball, making it the most-watched MLB game in the country's history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utes season ends with 76-68 loss to Indiana
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah women's basketball team's fourth consecutive trip to the NCAA Tournament was a short one. Yarden Garzon scored 17 points and Shay Ciezki added 16 as No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Guenther scores go-ahead goal in 3-2 win over Buffalo
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club scored three goals in the third period and defeated the Buffalo Sabres 5-2 at Delta Center on Thursday. Mikhail Sergachev scored twice for Utah (31-27-11), which has won five of its last seven games at home.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
